/**
 * Core module exports
 */

export { LinkrunnerSDK, sdk } from './sdk';
export { PayloadValidator, payloadValidator } from './validator';
export type { SDKInitOptions } from './sdk';
export { API_CONFIG, API_ROUTES, STORAGE_KEYS, SDK_CONSTANTS, ERROR_CODES } from './constants';

================
File: src/core/validator.ts
================
/**
 * Payload validation utilities
 */

import { InitPayload } from '../types';
import { logger } from '../utils/logger';

export class PayloadValidator {
  private static instance: PayloadValidator;

  private constructor() {}

  static getInstance(): PayloadValidator {
    if (!PayloadValidator.instance) {
      PayloadValidator.instance = new PayloadValidator();
    }
    return PayloadValidator.instance;
  }

  /**
   * Validate init payload
   */
  validateInitPayload(payload: Partial<InitPayload>): { isValid: boolean; errors: string[] } {
    const errors: string[] = [];

    // Required fields validation
    if (!payload.token || typeof payload.token !== 'string' || payload.token.trim() === '') {
      errors.push('token is required and must be a non-empty string');
    }

    if (!payload.package_version || typeof payload.package_version !== 'string') {
      errors.push('package_version is required and must be a string');
    }

    if (!payload.app_version || typeof payload.app_version !== 'string') {
      errors.push('app_version is required and must be a string');
    }

    if (!payload.device_data || typeof payload.device_data !== 'object') {
      errors.push('device_data is required and must be an object');
    }

    if (!payload.platform || typeof payload.platform !== 'string') {
      errors.push('platform is required and must be a string');
    }

    if (!payload.install_instance_id || typeof payload.install_instance_id !== 'string') {
      errors.push('install_instance_id is required and must be a string');
    }

    // Optional fields validation
    if (payload.link !== undefined && payload.link !== null && typeof payload.link !== 'string') {
      errors.push('link must be a string or null');
    }

    if (payload.source !== undefined && payload.source !== null && typeof payload.source !== 'string') {
      errors.push('source must be a string or null');
    }

    if (payload.debug !== undefined && typeof payload.debug !== 'boolean') {
      errors.push('debug must be a boolean');
    }

    // Additional validations
    if (payload.token && payload.token.length > 1000) {
      errors.push('token is too long (max 1000 characters)');
    }

    if (payload.package_version && !this.isValidVersion(payload.package_version)) {
      errors.push('package_version must be a valid version string (e.g., 1.0.0)');
    }

    if (payload.app_version && !this.isValidVersion(payload.app_version)) {
      errors.push('app_version must be a valid version string (e.g., 1.0.0)');
    }

    if (payload.platform && payload.platform.length > 100) {
      errors.push('platform is too long (max 100 characters)');
    }

    if (payload.install_instance_id && payload.install_instance_id.length > 100) {
      errors.push('install_instance_id is too long (max 100 characters)');
    }

    if (payload.link && !this.isValidURL(payload.link)) {
      errors.push('link must be a valid URL');
    }

    if (errors.length > 0) {
      logger.error('Init payload validation failed', { payload, errors });
    }

    return {
      isValid: errors.length === 0,
      errors,
    };
  }

  /**
   * Validate version string (semantic versioning)
   */
  private isValidVersion(version: string): boolean {
    const versionRegex = /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+(?:-[a-zA-Z0-9-]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9-]+)*)?(?:\+[a-zA-Z0-9-]+(?:\.[a-zA-Z0-9-]+)*)?$/;
    return versionRegex.test(version);
  }

  /**
   * Validate URL string
   */
  private isValidURL(url: string): boolean {
    try {
      new URL(url);
      return true;
    } catch {
      return false;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Sanitize payload by removing invalid characters and trimming
   */
  sanitizeInitPayload(payload: Partial<InitPayload>): Partial<InitPayload> {
    const sanitized: Partial<InitPayload> = {};

    // Sanitize string fields
    if (payload.token) {
      sanitized.token = payload.token.trim();
    }

    if (payload.package_version) {
      sanitized.package_version = payload.package_version.trim();
    }

    if (payload.app_version) {
      sanitized.app_version = payload.app_version.trim();
    }

    if (payload.platform) {
      sanitized.platform = payload.platform.trim();
    }

    if (payload.install_instance_id) {
      sanitized.install_instance_id = payload.install_instance_id.trim();
    }

    if (payload.link) {
      sanitized.link = payload.link.trim();
    }

    if (payload.source) {
      sanitized.source = payload.source.trim();
    }

    // Copy other fields as-is
    if (payload.device_data) {
      sanitized.device_data = payload.device_data;
    }

    if (payload.debug !== undefined) {
      sanitized.debug = payload.debug;
    }

    return sanitized;
  }

  /**
   * Create a complete init payload with defaults
   */
  createCompletePayload(
    partial: Partial<InitPayload>,
    deviceData: Record<string, any>
  ): InitPayload {
    const sanitized = this.sanitizeInitPayload(partial);

    return {
      token: sanitized.token || '',
      package_version: sanitized.package_version || '1.0.0',
      app_version: sanitized.app_version || '1.0.0',
      device_data: sanitized.device_data || deviceData,
      platform: sanitized.platform || 'web',
      install_instance_id: sanitized.install_instance_id || '',
      link: sanitized.link || null,
      source: sanitized.source || null,
      debug: sanitized.debug || false,
    };
  }
}

// Export singleton instance
export const payloadValidator = PayloadValidator.getInstance();

================
File: src/network/http-client.ts
================
/**
 * HTTP Client for API communication
 */

import { HTTPResponse, HTTPError } from '../types';
import { logger } from '../utils/logger';

export interface RequestOptions {
  method?: 'GET' | 'POST' | 'PUT' | 'DELETE';
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
  body?: any;
  timeout?: number;
}

export class HTTPClient {
  private static instance: HTTPClient;
  private defaultTimeout = 10000; // 10 seconds
  private defaultHeaders = {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  };

  private constructor() {}

  static getInstance(): HTTPClient {
    if (!HTTPClient.instance) {
      HTTPClient.instance = new HTTPClient();
    }
    return HTTPClient.instance;
  }

  /**
   * Make HTTP request with timeout and error handling
   */
  async request<T = any>(url: string, options: RequestOptions = {}): Promise<HTTPResponse<T>> {
    const {
      method = 'GET',
      headers = {},
      body,
      timeout = this.defaultTimeout,
    } = options;

    const requestHeaders = {
      ...this.defaultHeaders,
      ...headers,
    };

    // Create AbortController for timeout
    const controller = new AbortController();
    const timeoutId = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeout);

    try {
      logger.debug(`Making ${method} request to ${url}`, { headers: requestHeaders, body });

      const response = await fetch(url, {
        method,
        headers: requestHeaders,
        body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,
        signal: controller.signal,
      });

      clearTimeout(timeoutId);

      const responseHeaders: Record<string, string> = {};
      response.headers.forEach((value, key) => {
        responseHeaders[key] = value;
      });

      let data: T;
      const contentType = response.headers.get('content-type');
      
      if (contentType && contentType.includes('application/json')) {
        data = await response.json();
      } else {
        data = (await response.text()) as any;
      }

      const result: HTTPResponse<T> = {
        data,
        status: response.status,
        statusText: response.statusText,
        headers: responseHeaders,
      };

      if (!response.ok) {
        const error: HTTPError = new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}: ${response.statusText}`) as HTTPError;
        error.status = response.status;
        error.statusText = response.statusText;
        error.response = result;
        
        logger.error('HTTP request failed', error);
        throw error;
      }

      logger.debug(`Request successful`, { status: response.status, data });
      return result;

    } catch (error) {
      clearTimeout(timeoutId);

      if (error instanceof Error && error.name === 'AbortError') {
        const timeoutError: HTTPError = new Error(`Request timeout after ${timeout}ms`) as HTTPError;
        timeoutError.name = 'TimeoutError';
        logger.error('Request timed out', timeoutError);
        throw timeoutError;
      }

      if (error instanceof Error && (error as HTTPError).status) {
        // Already an HTTPError, just re-throw
        throw error;
      }

      // Network or other error
      const networkError: HTTPError = new Error('Network request failed') as HTTPError;
      networkError.name = 'NetworkError';
      logger.error('Network request failed', error);
      throw networkError;
    }
  }

  /**
   * GET request
   */
  async get<T = any>(url: string, options: Omit<RequestOptions, 'method' | 'body'> = {}): Promise<HTTPResponse<T>> {
    return this.request<T>(url, { ...options, method: 'GET' });
  }

  /**
   * POST request
   */
  async post<T = any>(url: string, body?: any, options: Omit<RequestOptions, 'method' | 'body'> = {}): Promise<HTTPResponse<T>> {
    return this.request<T>(url, { ...options, method: 'POST', body });
  }

  /**
   * PUT request
   */
  async put<T = any>(url: string, body?: any, options: Omit<RequestOptions, 'method' | 'body'> = {}): Promise<HTTPResponse<T>> {
    return this.request<T>(url, { ...options, method: 'PUT', body });
  }

  /**
   * DELETE request
   */
  async delete<T = any>(url: string, options: Omit<RequestOptions, 'method' | 'body'> = {}): Promise<HTTPResponse<T>> {
    return this.request<T>(url, { ...options, method: 'DELETE' });
  }

  /**
   * Set default timeout for all requests
   */
  setDefaultTimeout(timeout: number): void {
    this.defaultTimeout = timeout;
  }

  /**
   * Set default headers for all requests
   */
  setDefaultHeaders(headers: Record<string, string>): void {
    this.defaultHeaders = { ...this.defaultHeaders, ...headers };
  }

  /**
   * Clear default headers
   */
  clearDefaultHeaders(): void {
    this.defaultHeaders = {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    };
  }
}

// Export singleton instance
export const httpClient = HTTPClient.getInstance();

================
File: src/network/index.ts
================
/**
 * Network layer exports
 */

export { HTTPClient, httpClient } from './http-client';
export type { RequestOptions } from './http-client';

================
File: src/test/setup.ts
================
/**
 * Jest test setup file
 * This file is executed before each test file
 */

// Mock global objects that might not be available in jsdom
Object.defineProperty(window, 'location', {
  value: {
    href: 'http://localhost/',
    origin: 'http://localhost',
    hostname: 'localhost',
    pathname: '/',
    search: '',
    hash: '',
  },
  writable: true,
});

// Mock console methods for testing
const originalConsole = global.console;
global.console = {
  ...originalConsole,
  log: jest.fn(),
  debug: jest.fn(),
  info: jest.fn(),
  warn: jest.fn(),
  error: jest.fn(),
};

// Mock fetch for HTTP client testing
global.fetch = jest.fn();

// Mock performance API
Object.defineProperty(window, 'performance', {
  value: {
    now: jest.fn(() => Date.now()),
    mark: jest.fn(),
    measure: jest.fn(),
  },
  writable: true,
});

// Mock localStorage
const localStorageMock = {
  getItem: jest.fn(),
  setItem: jest.fn(),
  removeItem: jest.fn(),
  clear: jest.fn(),
  length: 0,
  key: jest.fn(),
};
Object.defineProperty(window, 'localStorage', {
  value: localStorageMock,
  writable: true,
});

// Mock sessionStorage
Object.defineProperty(window, 'sessionStorage', {
  value: localStorageMock,
  writable: true,
});

// Reset all mocks before each test
beforeEach(() => {
  jest.clearAllMocks();
});

================
File: src/types/index.ts
================
/**
 * Type definitions for the LR Web SDK
 */

// SDK Configuration Types
export interface SDKConfig {
  apiUrl: string;
  debug: boolean;
  timeout: number;
  retryAttempts: number;
  retryDelay: number;
}

// Init API Payload Types
export interface InitPayload {
  token: string;
  package_version: string;
  app_version: string;
  device_data: Record<string, any>;
  platform: string;
  install_instance_id: string;
  link?: string | null;
  source?: string | null;
  debug?: boolean;
}

// Init API Response Types
export interface InitResponse {
  session_id: string;
  config: SDKConfig;
  features: Record<string, any>;
  endpoints: {
    events: string;
    config: string;
    heartbeat: string;
  };
}

// Page View Capture Types
export interface PageViewPayload {
  token: string;
  install_instance_id: string;
  link: string;
}

export interface PageViewResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message?: string;
}

// Device Data Types
export interface DeviceData {
  user_agent: string;
  screen_width: number;
  screen_height: number;
  viewport_width: number;
  viewport_height: number;
  device_type: 'mobile' | 'tablet' | 'desktop';
  browser_name: string;
  browser_version: string;
  os_name: string;
  os_version: string;
  language: string;
  timezone: string;
  pixel_ratio: number;
}

// SDK State Types
export type SDKState = 'uninitialized' | 'initializing' | 'ready' | 'error' | 'destroyed';

// Error Types
export interface SDKError {
  code: string;
  message: string;
  details?: any;
  timestamp: number;
}

// HTTP Client Types
export interface HTTPResponse<T = any> {
  data: T;
  status: number;
  statusText: string;
  headers: Record<string, string>;
}

export interface HTTPError extends Error {
  status?: number;
  statusText?: string;
  response?: any;
}

================
File: src/utils/device.ts
================
/**
 * Device detection and information utilities
 */

import { DeviceData } from '../types';

export class DeviceDetector {
  private static instance: DeviceDetector;

  private constructor() {}

  static getInstance(): DeviceDetector {
    if (!DeviceDetector.instance) {
      DeviceDetector.instance = new DeviceDetector();
    }
    return DeviceDetector.instance;
  }

  getDeviceData(): DeviceData {
    const userAgent = navigator.userAgent;
    const screen = window.screen;
    const viewport = this.getViewportDimensions();

    return {
      user_agent: userAgent,
      screen_width: screen.width,
      screen_height: screen.height,
      viewport_width: viewport.width,
      viewport_height: viewport.height,
      device_type: this.getDeviceType(),
      browser_name: this.getBrowserName(),
      browser_version: this.getBrowserVersion(),
      os_name: this.getOSName(),
      os_version: this.getOSVersion(),
      language: navigator.language || 'unknown',
      timezone: Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone || 'unknown',
      pixel_ratio: window.devicePixelRatio || 1,
    };
  }

  private getViewportDimensions(): { width: number; height: number } {
    return {
      width: window.innerWidth || document.documentElement.clientWidth || 0,
      height: window.innerHeight || document.documentElement.clientHeight || 0,
    };
  }

  private getDeviceType(): 'mobile' | 'tablet' | 'desktop' {
    const userAgent = navigator.userAgent.toLowerCase();
    const viewport = this.getViewportDimensions();

    // Check for mobile indicators
    if (
      /mobile|android|iphone|ipod|blackberry|iemobile|opera mini/i.test(
        userAgent
      )
    ) {
      return 'mobile';
    }

    // Check for tablet indicators
    if (/tablet|ipad/i.test(userAgent)) {
      return 'tablet';
    }

    // Check viewport size as fallback
    if (viewport.width <= 768) {
      return 'mobile';
    } else if (viewport.width <= 1024) {
      return 'tablet';
    }

    return 'desktop';
  }

  private getBrowserName(): string {
    const userAgent = navigator.userAgent;

    if (userAgent.includes('Chrome') && !userAgent.includes('Edg')) {
      return 'Chrome';
    } else if (userAgent.includes('Firefox')) {
      return 'Firefox';
    } else if (userAgent.includes('Safari') && !userAgent.includes('Chrome')) {
      return 'Safari';
    } else if (userAgent.includes('Edg')) {
      return 'Edge';
    } else if (userAgent.includes('MSIE') || userAgent.includes('Trident')) {
      return 'Internet Explorer';
    }

    return 'Unknown';
  }

  private getBrowserVersion(): string {
    const userAgent = navigator.userAgent;
    let match: RegExpMatchArray | null = null;

    // Chrome
    if (userAgent.includes('Chrome') && !userAgent.includes('Edg')) {
      match = userAgent.match(/Chrome\/(\d+)/);
    }
    // Firefox
    else if (userAgent.includes('Firefox')) {
      match = userAgent.match(/Firefox\/(\d+)/);
    }
    // Safari
    else if (userAgent.includes('Safari') && !userAgent.includes('Chrome')) {
      match = userAgent.match(/Version\/(\d+)/);
    }
    // Edge
    else if (userAgent.includes('Edg')) {
      match = userAgent.match(/Edg\/(\d+)/);
    }

    return match ? match[1] : 'Unknown';
  }

  private getOSName(): string {
    const userAgent = navigator.userAgent;

    if (userAgent.includes('Windows')) return 'Windows';
    if (userAgent.includes('Mac')) return 'macOS';
    if (userAgent.includes('Linux')) return 'Linux';
    if (userAgent.includes('Android')) return 'Android';
    if (userAgent.includes('iPhone') || userAgent.includes('iPad'))
      return 'iOS';

    return 'Unknown';
  }

  private getOSVersion(): string {
    const userAgent = navigator.userAgent;
    let match: RegExpMatchArray | null = null;

    if (userAgent.includes('Windows')) {
      match = userAgent.match(/Windows NT (\d+\.\d+)/);
    } else if (userAgent.includes('Mac')) {
      match = userAgent.match(/Mac OS X (\d+[._]\d+)/);
    } else if (userAgent.includes('Android')) {
      match = userAgent.match(/Android (\d+)/);
    } else if (userAgent.includes('iPhone') || userAgent.includes('iPad')) {
      match = userAgent.match(/OS (\d+[._]\d+)/);
    }

    return match ? match[1].replace(/_/g, '.') : 'Unknown';
  }
}

// Export singleton instance
export const deviceDetector = DeviceDetector.getInstance();

================
File: src/utils/logger.ts
================
/**
 * Simple logger utility for debugging and development
 */

export type LogLevel = 'debug' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error';

export class Logger {
  private static instance: Logger;
  private logLevel: LogLevel = 'warn';
  private enabled = false;

  private constructor() {}

  static getInstance(): Logger {
    if (!Logger.instance) {
      Logger.instance = new Logger();
    }
    return Logger.instance;
  }

  setLevel(level: LogLevel): void {
    this.logLevel = level;
  }

  setEnabled(enabled: boolean): void {
    this.enabled = enabled;
  }

  private shouldLog(level: LogLevel): boolean {
    if (!this.enabled) return false;

    const levels: Record<LogLevel, number> = {
      debug: 0,
      info: 1,
      warn: 2,
      error: 3,
    };

    return levels[level] >= levels[this.logLevel];
  }

  private formatMessage(level: LogLevel, message: string, ...args: any[]): any[] {
    const timestamp = new Date().toISOString();
    const prefix = `[Linkrunner-SDK][${timestamp}][${level.toUpperCase()}]`;
    return [prefix, message, ...args];
  }

  debug(message: string, ...args: any[]): void {
    if (this.shouldLog('debug')) {
      console.debug(...this.formatMessage('debug', message, ...args));
    }
  }

  info(message: string, ...args: any[]): void {
    if (this.shouldLog('info')) {
      console.info(...this.formatMessage('info', message, ...args));
    }
  }

  warn(message: string, ...args: any[]): void {
    if (this.shouldLog('warn')) {
      console.warn(...this.formatMessage('warn', message, ...args));
    }
  }

  error(message: string, ...args: any[]): void {
    if (this.shouldLog('error')) {
      console.error(...this.formatMessage('error', message, ...args));
    }
  }
}

// Export singleton instance
export const logger = Logger.getInstance();

================
File: src/utils/storage.ts
================
/**
 * Storage utilities with fallbacks and error handling
 */

import { logger } from './logger';

export type StorageType = 'localStorage' | 'sessionStorage' | 'memory';

export class StorageManager {
  private static instance: StorageManager;
  private memoryStorage: Map<string, string> = new Map();

  private constructor() {}

  static getInstance(): StorageManager {
    if (!StorageManager.instance) {
      StorageManager.instance = new StorageManager();
    }
    return StorageManager.instance;
  }

  /**
   * Check if storage type is available
   */
  isStorageAvailable(type: StorageType): boolean {
    if (type === 'memory') return true;

    try {
      const storage = window[type];
      const testKey = '__linkrunner_sdk_test__';
      storage.setItem(testKey, 'test');
      storage.removeItem(testKey);
      return true;
    } catch {
      return false;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get the best available storage type
   */
  getBestStorageType(): StorageType {
    if (this.isStorageAvailable('localStorage')) {
      return 'localStorage';
    }
    if (this.isStorageAvailable('sessionStorage')) {
      return 'sessionStorage';
    }
    return 'memory';
  }

  /**
   * Set item in storage with fallback
   */
  setItem(
    key: string,
    value: string,
    preferredType?: StorageType
  ): StorageType {
    const storageType = preferredType || this.getBestStorageType();

    try {
      if (storageType === 'memory') {
        this.memoryStorage.set(key, value);
        return 'memory';
      }

      const storage = window[storageType];
      storage.setItem(key, value);
      return storageType;
    } catch (error) {
      logger.warn(`Failed to set item in ${storageType}, falling back`, error);

      // Fallback to memory storage
      this.memoryStorage.set(key, value);
      return 'memory';
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get item from storage with fallback
   */
  getItem(key: string, preferredType?: StorageType): string | null {
    const storageType = preferredType || this.getBestStorageType();

    try {
      if (storageType === 'memory') {
        return this.memoryStorage.get(key) || null;
      }

      const storage = window[storageType];
      return storage.getItem(key);
    } catch (error) {
      logger.warn(`Failed to get item from ${storageType}, trying memory`, error);

      // Fallback to memory storage
      return this.memoryStorage.get(key) || null;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Remove item from storage
   */
  removeItem(key: string, preferredType?: StorageType): void {
    const storageType = preferredType || this.getBestStorageType();

    try {
      if (storageType === 'memory') {
        this.memoryStorage.delete(key);
        return;
      }

      const storage = window[storageType];
      storage.removeItem(key);
    } catch (error) {
      logger.warn(`Failed to remove item from ${storageType}`, error);
    }

    // Always try to remove from memory storage as well
    this.memoryStorage.delete(key);
  }

  /**
   * Clear all items from storage
   */
  clear(preferredType?: StorageType): void {
    const storageType = preferredType || this.getBestStorageType();

    try {
      if (storageType === 'memory') {
        this.memoryStorage.clear();
        return;
      }

      const storage = window[storageType];
      // Only clear items with our prefix to avoid affecting other applications
      const keysToRemove: string[] = [];
      for (let i = 0; i < storage.length; i++) {
        const key = storage.key(i);
        if (key && key.startsWith('linkrunner_sdk_')) {
          keysToRemove.push(key);
        }
      }
      keysToRemove.forEach(key => storage.removeItem(key));
    } catch (error) {
      logger.warn(`Failed to clear ${storageType}`, error);
    }

    // Always clear memory storage
    this.memoryStorage.clear();
  }

  /**
   * Set object as JSON string
   */
  setObject<T>(
    key: string,
    value: T,
    preferredType?: StorageType
  ): StorageType {
    try {
      const jsonString = JSON.stringify(value);
      return this.setItem(key, jsonString, preferredType);
    } catch (error) {
      logger.error('Failed to serialize object for storage', error);
      throw new Error('Failed to serialize object for storage');
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get object from JSON string
   */
  getObject<T>(key: string, preferredType?: StorageType): T | null {
    try {
      const jsonString = this.getItem(key, preferredType);
      if (!jsonString) return null;

      return JSON.parse(jsonString) as T;
    } catch (error) {
      logger.warn('Failed to parse stored JSON object', error);
      return null;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get storage usage info
   */
  getStorageInfo(): {
    localStorage: { available: boolean; used?: number; total?: number };
    sessionStorage: { available: boolean; used?: number; total?: number };
    memory: { available: boolean; used: number };
  } {
    const info = {
      localStorage: { available: false as boolean, used: undefined as number | undefined, total: undefined as number | undefined },
      sessionStorage: { available: false as boolean, used: undefined as number | undefined, total: undefined as number | undefined },
      memory: { available: true, used: this.memoryStorage.size },
    };

    // Check localStorage
    if (this.isStorageAvailable('localStorage')) {
      info.localStorage.available = true;
      try {
        const used = JSON.stringify(localStorage).length;
        info.localStorage.used = used;
        // Try to estimate total by attempting to use storage
        // This is approximate and may not be accurate
      } catch {
        // Ignore errors
      }
    }

    // Check sessionStorage
    if (this.isStorageAvailable('sessionStorage')) {
      info.sessionStorage.available = true;
      try {
        const used = JSON.stringify(sessionStorage).length;
        info.sessionStorage.used = used;
      } catch {
        // Ignore errors
      }
    }

    return info;
  }
}

// Export singleton instance
export const storageManager = StorageManager.getInstance();

================
File: src/utils/url.ts
================
/**
 * URL parsing and manipulation utilities
 */

export class URLParser {
  private static instance: URLParser;

  private constructor() {}

  static getInstance(): URLParser {
    if (!URLParser.instance) {
      URLParser.instance = new URLParser();
    }
    return URLParser.instance;
  }

  /**
   * Get current page URL
   */
  getCurrentURL(): string {
    return window.location.href;
  }

  /**
   * Get current page pathname
   */
  getCurrentPath(): string {
    return window.location.pathname;
  }

  /**
   * Get current page hostname
   */
  getCurrentHostname(): string {
    return window.location.hostname;
  }

  /**
   * Get current page protocol
   */
  getCurrentProtocol(): string {
    return window.location.protocol;
  }

  /**
   * Parse query parameters from URL
   */
  parseQueryParams(url?: string): Record<string, string> {
    const targetURL = url || window.location.href;
    const params: Record<string, string> = {};

    try {
      const urlObj = new URL(targetURL);
      urlObj.searchParams.forEach((value, key) => {
        params[key] = value;
      });
    } catch (error) {
      // Fallback for malformed URLs
      const queryString = targetURL.split('?')[1];
      if (queryString) {
        queryString.split('&').forEach(param => {
          const [key, value] = param.split('=');
          if (key) {
            params[decodeURIComponent(key)] = value
              ? decodeURIComponent(value)
              : '';
          }
        });
      }
    }

    return params;
  }

  /**
   * Get specific query parameter value
   */
  getQueryParam(name: string, url?: string): string | null {
    const params = this.parseQueryParams(url);
    return params[name] || null;
  }

  /**
   * Check if URL is valid
   */
  isValidURL(url: string): boolean {
    try {
      new URL(url);
      return true;
    } catch {
      return false;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get domain from URL
   */
  getDomain(url?: string): string {
    const targetURL = url || window.location.href;
    try {
      const urlObj = new URL(targetURL);
      return urlObj.hostname;
    } catch {
      return '';
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get subdomain from URL
   */
  getSubdomain(url?: string): string {
    const domain = this.getDomain(url);
    const parts = domain.split('.');

    // If there are more than 2 parts, the first part is likely a subdomain
    if (parts.length > 2) {
      return parts[0];
    }

    return '';
  }

  /**
   * Get referrer URL
   */
  getReferrer(): string {
    return document.referrer || '';
  }

  /**
   * Get page title
   */
  getPageTitle(): string {
    return document.title || '';
  }

  /**
   * Normalize URL (remove fragments, sort query params)
   */
  normalizeURL(url: string): string {
    try {
      const urlObj = new URL(url);
      // Remove fragment
      urlObj.hash = '';
      // Sort query parameters
      const sortedParams = new URLSearchParams();
      Array.from(urlObj.searchParams.keys())
        .sort()
        .forEach(key => {
          const values = urlObj.searchParams.getAll(key);
          values.forEach(value => sortedParams.append(key, value));
        });
      urlObj.search = sortedParams.toString();
      return urlObj.toString();
    } catch {
      return url;
    }
  }
}

// Export singleton instance
export const urlParser = URLParser.getInstance();

================
File: ARCHITECTURE.md
================
# Web SDK Architecture

This document provides a comprehensive architectural overview of the Web SDK for capturing web events, analytics, and advertising data.

## 1. System Overview

The Web SDK is a lightweight, platform-agnostic JavaScript library that captures web events and sends them to a data collection server. It's designed to be embedded as a single script tag in web pages.

## 2. High-Level Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        Client Website                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                    Web Page                             │    │
│  │  ┌───────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ │    │
│  │  │   HTML Head   │  │         Web SDK Script         │ │    │
│  │  │ <script src=  │  │ ┌─────────────────────────────┐ │ │    │
│  │  │ "sdk.min.js"> │  │ │      Event Collectors       │ │ │    │
│  │  │ </script>     │  │ │ • Page View                 │ │ │    │
│  │  └───────────────┘  │ │ • Query Params              │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • GCLID                     │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • Domain/Subdomain          │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • Custom Events             │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • User Data                 │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ └─────────────────────────────┘ │ │    │
│  │                     │ ┌─────────────────────────────┐ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │      Data Processor         │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • Validation                │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • Enrichment                │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • Queuing                   │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ └─────────────────────────────┘ │ │    │
│  │                     │ ┌─────────────────────────────┐ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │    Network Transport        │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • HTTP Client               │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • Retry Logic               │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ │ • Error Handling            │ │ │    │
│  │                     │ └─────────────────────────────┘ │ │    │
│  │                     └─────────────────────────────────┘ │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
                                    │ HTTPS
                                    ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Data Collection Server                      │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                     API Gateway                         │    │
│  │ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ │    │
│  │ │ Authentication  │ │ Rate Limiting   │ │ Validation  │ │    │
│  │ └─────────────────┘ └─────────────────┘ └─────────────┘ │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                Event Processing Layer                   │    │
│  │ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ │    │
│  │ │ Event Router    │ │ Data Enricher   │ │ Transformer │ │    │
│  │ └─────────────────┘ └─────────────────┘ └─────────────┘ │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                  Data Storage Layer                     │    │
│  │ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ │    │
│  │ │   Time Series   │ │   Event Store   │ │   Analytics │ │    │
│  │ │    Database     │ │   (NoSQL/SQL)   │ │   Database  │ │    │
│  │ └─────────────────┘ └─────────────────┘ └─────────────┘ │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 3. Component Architecture

### 3.1 SDK Core Components

```
Web SDK Architecture
├── Core
│   ├── SDK Initializer
│   ├── Configuration Manager
│   └── Instance Manager
├── Event Collectors
│   ├── Page View Collector
│   ├── Query Parameter Collector
│   ├── GCLID Collector
│   ├── Domain Collector
│   ├── Custom Event Collector
│   └── User Data Collector
├── Data Processing
│   ├── Event Validator
│   ├── Data Enricher
│   ├── Event Queue
│   └── Privacy Manager
├── Network Layer
│   ├── HTTP Client
│   ├── Retry Manager
│   ├── Offline Storage
│   └── Error Handler
└── Utilities
    ├── Device Detector
    ├── URL Parser
    ├── Storage Manager
    └── Logger
```

#### Core Layer
**SDK Initializer**
- Entry point for SDK activation and setup
- Handles the `POST {{url}}/api/client/init` API call with initialization payload
- Orchestrates component bootstrapping sequence
- Manages async initialization to prevent blocking page load
- Sets up global SDK namespace and prevents multiple instances
- Validates required configuration parameters (token, platform, etc.)

**Configuration Manager**
- Manages SDK configuration state and runtime settings
- Handles dynamic configuration updates from server
- Stores feature flags, sampling rates, and endpoint configurations
- Provides fallback configurations for offline scenarios
- Validates configuration schema and applies defaults
- Manages debug mode settings and logging levels

**Instance Manager**
- Implements singleton pattern to ensure single SDK instance per page
- Manages SDK lifecycle states (initializing, ready, error, destroyed)
- Handles SDK teardown and cleanup on page unload
- Coordinates communication between SDK components
- Manages global event listeners and cleanup
- Provides SDK health status and diagnostic information

#### Event Collectors Layer
**Page View Collector**
- Automatically captures page navigation events on load and SPA route changes
- Extracts page metadata (title, URL, referrer, timestamp)
- Handles browser history API integration for single-page applications
- Manages page session tracking and visit duration
- Captures performance metrics (load time, DOM ready time)

**Query Parameter Collector**
- Parses and extracts all URL query parameters
- Filters sensitive parameters based on configuration
- Handles URL decoding and parameter validation
- Tracks campaign parameters (utm_*, fbclid, etc.)
- Manages parameter persistence across page navigation

**GCLID Collector**
- Specialized collector for Google Click ID tracking
- Extracts and validates gclid parameter from URL
- Manages GCLID persistence in local storage
- Handles GCLID expiration and cleanup
- Integrates with Google Ads conversion tracking requirements

**Domain Collector**
- Identifies and captures domain and subdomain information
- Extracts hostname, protocol, and port details
- Manages cross-domain tracking scenarios
- Handles internationalized domain names (IDN)
- Captures referrer domain for traffic source analysis

**Custom Event Collector**
- Provides API for developers to track custom events
- Manages event schema validation and parameter sanitization
- Handles event queuing and rate limiting
- Supports event metadata and custom properties
- Implements event naming conventions and validation

**User Data Collector**
- Captures user interaction data (clicks, form submissions, scrolling)
- Manages user session tracking and identification
- Handles privacy-compliant data collection
- Implements data minimization and anonymization
- Tracks user engagement metrics and behavior patterns

#### Data Processing Layer
**Event Validator**
- Validates event schema and data types
- Ensures data integrity and format compliance
- Performs data sanitization and cleaning
- Handles malformed data gracefully
- Implements validation rules and constraints

**Data Enricher**
- Adds contextual information to events (timestamps, session IDs, user agent)
- Enriches events with device and browser information
- Adds geolocation data (if permitted)
- Manages session state and user identification
- Implements data transformation and normalization

**Event Queue**
- Manages individual event queuing for transmission
- Implements event prioritization and ordering
- Handles event storage and retrieval
- Manages queue size limits and cleanup
- Optimizes single event transmission

**Privacy Manager**
- Handles GDPR/CCPA compliance and consent management
- Implements data masking and PII filtering
- Manages opt-out and data deletion requests
- Handles cookie consent and tracking preferences
- Ensures compliance with regional privacy regulations

#### Network Layer
**HTTP Client**
- Manages all API communication with the data collection server
- Implements request/response handling and serialization
- Handles authentication and authorization headers
- Manages request timeouts and connection pooling
- Implements request/response interceptors

**Retry Manager**
- Implements exponential backoff for failed requests
- Manages retry policies and maximum attempt limits
- Handles different error types with appropriate retry strategies
- Implements jitter to prevent thundering herd problems
- Manages circuit breaker patterns for server failures

**Offline Storage**
- Queues events when network is unavailable
- Implements persistent storage using IndexedDB/localStorage
- Manages storage quotas and cleanup policies
- Handles storage serialization and compression
- Implements storage encryption for sensitive data

**Error Handler**
- Manages and reports SDK errors and exceptions
- Implements error categorization and severity levels
- Handles graceful degradation for non-critical errors
- Manages error reporting to monitoring systems
- Implements error recovery and fallback mechanisms

#### Utilities Layer
**Device Detector**
- Identifies device type, browser, and operating system
- Extracts screen resolution and viewport information
- Detects mobile vs desktop environments
- Handles user agent parsing and device fingerprinting
- Manages device capability detection

**URL Parser**
- Provides URL parsing and manipulation utilities
- Handles query parameter extraction and encoding
- Manages URL validation and normalization
- Supports relative and absolute URL handling
- Implements URL fragment and hash parsing

**Storage Manager**
- Abstracts browser storage APIs (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
- Implements storage fallbacks and feature detection
- Manages storage encryption and security
- Handles storage quota management and cleanup
- Provides consistent storage interface across browsers

**Logger**
- Implements structured logging with multiple levels (debug, info, warn, error)
- Manages log formatting and output destinations
- Handles log filtering based on configuration
- Implements log buffering and batching
- Provides debugging tools and diagnostic information

### 3.2 Detailed Component Responsibilities

#### Core Components
- **SDK Initializer**: Bootstraps the SDK with configuration
- **Configuration Manager**: Manages SDK settings and feature flags
- **Instance Manager**: Handles SDK lifecycle and singleton pattern

#### Event Collectors
- **Page View Collector**: Captures page navigation events
- **Query Parameter Collector**: Extracts and processes URL parameters
- **GCLID Collector**: Specifically handles Google Click ID for ad attribution
- **Domain Collector**: Identifies domain and subdomain information
- **Custom Event Collector**: Processes user-defined events
- **User Data Collector**: Gathers user interaction data

#### Data Processing
- **Event Validator**: Ensures data integrity and format compliance
- **Data Enricher**: Adds contextual information (timestamps, session IDs)
- **Event Queue**: Manages individual event queuing for transmission
- **Privacy Manager**: Handles GDPR/CCPA compliance and data masking

#### Network Layer
- **HTTP Client**: Manages API communication
- **Retry Manager**: Implements exponential backoff for failed requests
- **Offline Storage**: Queues events when network is unavailable
- **Error Handler**: Manages and reports SDK errors

## 4. Sequence Diagrams

### 4.1 SDK Initialization Flow

```
User Browser    Web Page    SDK Script    Config Manager    Event Collectors    Network Client
     │              │           │              │                   │                │
     │──────────────▶│           │              │                   │                │
     │   Load Page   │           │              │                   │                │
     │              │───────────▶│              │                   │                │
     │              │ Load SDK   │              │                   │                │
     │              │           │──────────────▶│                   │                │
     │              │           │  Initialize   │                   │                │
     │              │           │              │───────────────────▶│                │
     │              │           │              │  Setup Collectors  │                │
     │              │           │              │                   │────────────────▶│
     │              │           │              │                   │ Setup Network   │
     │              │           │◀──────────────│                   │                │
     │              │           │   Ready       │                   │                │
     │              │◀───────────│              │                   │                │
     │              │   Ready    │              │                   │                │
     │◀──────────────│           │              │                   │                │
     │   Page Ready  │           │              │                   │                │
```

### 4.2 Event Capture and Transmission Flow

```
User Action    Event Collector    Data Processor    Network Client    Server API
     │               │                 │                │              │
     │───────────────▶│                 │                │              │
     │   Page View    │                 │                │              │
     │               │─────────────────▶│                │              │
     │               │  Capture Event   │                │              │
     │               │                 │────────────────▶│              │
     │               │                 │  Queue Event    │              │
     │               │                 │                │──────────────▶│
     │               │                 │                │   Send Event  │
     │               │                 │                │◀──────────────│
     │               │                 │                │   Response    │
     │               │                 │◀────────────────│              │
     │               │                 │  Confirm Sent   │              │
     │               │◀─────────────────│                │              │
     │               │   Success        │                │              │
```

### 4.3 Error Handling and Retry Flow

```
Network Client    Retry Manager    Offline Storage    Event Queue    Server API
     │                  │               │               │             │
     │──────────────────▶│               │               │             │
     │   Send Failed     │               │               │             │
     │                  │───────────────▶│               │             │
     │                  │  Store Event   │               │             │
     │                  │               │───────────────▶│             │
     │                  │               │  Add to Queue  │             │
     │                  │               │               │             │
     │                  │◀───────── Retry Timer ─────────│             │
     │                  │               │               │             │
     │◀──────────────────│               │               │             │
     │   Retry Send      │               │               │             │
     │──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────▶│
     │                                                   Send Retry   │
     │◀──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
     │                                                   Success      │
     │                  │               │◀───────────────│             │
     │                  │               │ Remove from Q  │             │
```

## 5. Deployment and Distribution Architecture

### 5.1 Build and Distribution Pipeline

```
Development → Build Process → CDN Distribution → Client Integration
     │             │              │                    │
     │             │              │                    │
┌────▼────┐   ┌────▼────┐    ┌────▼────┐         ┌────▼────┐
│TypeScript│   │ Rollup  │    │   CDN   │         │ Client  │
│ Source   │──▶│ Bundle  │───▶│ Global  │────────▶│Website  │
│ Code     │   │         │    │Distribution│      │         │
└─────────┘   └─────────┘    └─────────┘         └─────────┘
     │             │              │                    │
     │             │              │                    │
     ▼             ▼              ▼                    ▼
┌─────────┐   ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐         ┌─────────┐
│ Linting │   │Minify & │    │Regional │         │<script  │
│Testing  │   │Compress │    │Mirrors  │         │src="cdn"│
│Type     │   │Tree     │    │Caching  │         │></script│
│Check    │   │Shaking  │    │         │         │         │
└─────────┘   └─────────┘    └─────────┘         └─────────┘
```

### 5.2 CDN Distribution Strategy

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        CDN Network                          │
│                                                             │
│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐     │
│  │US-East      │    │US-West      │    │EU-Central   │     │
│  │Primary      │    │Secondary    │    │Regional     │     │
│  │Edge Server  │    │Edge Server  │    │Edge Server  │     │
│  └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘     │
│         │                   │                   │          │
│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐     │
│  │Asia-Pacific │    │South America│    │Africa/ME    │     │
│  │Regional     │    │Regional     │    │Regional     │     │
│  │Edge Server  │    │Edge Server  │    │Edge Server  │     │
│  └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   Client Requests                          │
│                                                             │
│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐     │
│  │Americas     │    │Europe       │    │Asia Pacific │     │
│  │Users        │    │Users        │    │Users        │     │
│  └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 6. Data Flow Architecture

### 6.1 Event Data Flow

```
Web Page Events → SDK Collection → Processing → Transmission → Server Storage
      │                │              │             │              │
      │                │              │             │              │
      ▼                ▼              ▼             ▼              ▼
┌──────────┐    ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────────┐
│• Page    │    │• Capture    │ │• Validate   │ │• Queue  │ │• Event      │
│  View    │───▶│• Enrich     │▶│• Transform  │▶│• Send   │▶│  Storage    │
│• Clicks  │    │• Filter     │ │• Sanitize   │ │• Retry  │ │• Analytics  │
│• Forms   │    │• Buffer     │ │• Compress   │ │• Store  │ │• Reporting  │
└──────────┘    └─────────────┘ └─────────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────────┘
```

### 6.2 API Integration Points

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      API Endpoints                         │
│                                                             │
│  POST {{url}}/api/client/init                              │
│  ├── SDK Initialization & Session Creation                 │
│  ├── Request Body: {                                       │
│  │     token: String,                                      │
│  │     package_version: String,                            │
│  │     app_version: String,                                │
│  │     device_data: Map<String, Any>,                      │
│  │     platform: String,                                   │
│  │     install_instance_id: String,                        │
│  │     link: String? = null,                               │
│  │     source: String? = null,                             │
│  │     debug: Boolean? = false                             │
│  │   }                                                     │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 7. Security Architecture

### 7.1 Data Security Flow

```
Client Data → Encryption → Transmission → Server Validation → Storage
     │             │           │               │                │
     ▼             ▼           ▼               ▼                ▼
┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────┐
│• PII    │  │• TLS 1.3│ │• HTTPS  │    │• API Key    │  │• Encrypted│
│  Masking│─▶│• Payload│▶│• Headers│───▶│  Validation │─▶│  Storage  │
│• Data   │  │  Encrypt│ │• Cert   │    │• Rate Limit │  │• Access   │
│  Filter │  │• Hash   │ │  Verify │    │• Schema     │  │  Control  │
└─────────┘  └─────────┘ └─────────┘    └─────────────┘  └─────────┘
```

## 8. Performance Considerations

### 8.1 SDK Performance Targets

- **Bundle Size**: < 25KB minified + gzipped
- **Initialization Time**: < 100ms
- **Event Collection Overhead**: < 5ms per event
- **Memory Footprint**: < 1MB
- **Network Efficiency**: Individual event transmission with queueing

### 8.2 Optimization Strategies

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   Performance Optimizations                │
│                                                             │
│  Frontend Optimizations          Backend Optimizations     │
│  ┌─────────────────────┐         ┌─────────────────────┐   │
│  │• Lazy Loading       │         │• Connection Pooling │   │
│  │• Tree Shaking       │         │• Request Batching   │   │
│  │• Code Splitting     │         │• Response Caching  │   │
│  │• Async Initialization│        │• Load Balancing     │   │
│  │• Event Debouncing   │         │• Auto Scaling       │   │
│  │• Memory Pooling     │         │• CDN Optimization   │   │
│  └─────────────────────┘         └─────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 9. Monitoring and Observability

### 9.1 SDK Health Monitoring

```
SDK Metrics → Client Telemetry → Monitoring Dashboard → Alerting
     │              │                    │                │
     ▼              ▼                    ▼                ▼
┌─────────┐   ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐   ┌─────────┐
│• Error  │   │• Performance│    │• Real-time  │   │• Error  │
│  Rates  │──▶│  Metrics    │───▶│  Dashboard  │──▶│  Alerts │
│• Usage  │   │• Health     │    │• Historical │   │• SLA    │
│  Stats  │   │  Checks     │    │  Analytics  │   │  Alerts │
└─────────┘   └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘   └─────────┘
```

This architecture provides a comprehensive foundation for building a robust, scalable, and maintainable Web SDK that meets all the specified requirements while ensuring optimal performance and reliability.

================
File: CDN_DISTRIBUTION_GUIDE.md
================
# CDN Distribution Guide - AWS S3 + CloudFront

This guide provides step-by-step instructions for distributing the Linkrunner Web SDK via AWS S3 and CloudFront CDN.

## Table of Contents

1. [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
2. [Step 1: Prepare SDK for Distribution](#step-1-prepare-sdk-for-distribution)
3. [Step 2: Create S3 Bucket](#step-2-create-s3-bucket)
4. [Step 3: Configure S3 for Static Website Hosting](#step-3-configure-s3-for-static-website-hosting)
5. [Step 4: Upload SDK Files to S3](#step-4-upload-sdk-files-to-s3)
6. [Step 5: Create CloudFront Distribution](#step-5-create-cloudfront-distribution)
7. [Step 6: Configure Custom Domain (Optional)](#step-6-configure-custom-domain-optional)
8. [Step 7: Test CDN Distribution](#step-7-test-cdn-distribution)
9. [Step 8: SDK Consumption Examples](#step-8-sdk-consumption-examples)
10. [Step 9: Version Management](#step-9-version-management)
11. [Step 10: Monitoring and Analytics](#step-10-monitoring-and-analytics)

## Prerequisites

- AWS Account with appropriate permissions
- AWS CLI installed and configured
- Domain name (optional, for custom CDN URL)
- Built SDK files in `dist/` directory

## Step 1: Prepare SDK for Distribution

### 1.1 Build Production Assets

```bash
# Clean previous builds
npm run clean

# Build production bundles
npm run build:prod

# Verify build output
ls -la dist/
```

### 1.2 Create Distribution Package

Create a distribution structure with versioning:

```bash
# Create distribution directory structure
mkdir -p distribution/v1.0.0
mkdir -p distribution/latest

# Copy build files with SRI hashes
cp dist/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js distribution/v1.0.0/
cp dist/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js distribution/v1.0.0/
cp dist/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js distribution/latest/
cp dist/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js distribution/latest/

# Generate minified versions if not already present
# (These should be created by the build process)
```

### 1.3 Generate SRI Hashes

```bash
# Generate integrity hashes for security
openssl dgst -sha384 -binary distribution/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js | openssl base64 -A
openssl dgst -sha384 -binary distribution/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js | openssl base64 -A
```

Save these hashes for later use in integrity attributes.

## Step 2: Create S3 Bucket

### 2.1 Create Bucket via AWS CLI

```bash
# Set variables
BUCKET_NAME="linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn"
REGION="us-east-1"

# Create S3 bucket
aws s3 mb s3://$BUCKET_NAME --region $REGION
```

### 2.2 Create Bucket via AWS Console

1. Go to AWS S3 Console
2. Click "Create bucket"
3. Bucket name: `linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn`
4. Region: `us-east-1` (recommended for CloudFront)
5. Uncheck "Block all public access"
6. Acknowledge the warning
7. Click "Create bucket"

## Step 3: Configure S3 for Static Website Hosting

### 3.1 Set Bucket Policy

Create a bucket policy file:

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "PublicReadGetObject",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Action": "s3:GetObject",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/*"
    }
  ]
}
```

Apply the policy:

```bash
# Save the policy to bucket-policy.json
aws s3api put-bucket-policy --bucket linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn --policy file://bucket-policy.json
```

### 3.2 Enable Static Website Hosting

```bash
aws s3 website s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn --index-document index.html --error-document error.html
```

## Step 4: Upload SDK Files to S3

### 4.1 Upload with Proper Headers

```bash
# Upload with cache control and content type headers
aws s3 cp distribution/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/v1.0.0/ \
  --content-type "application/javascript" \
  --cache-control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" \
  --metadata-directive REPLACE

aws s3 cp distribution/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/v1.0.0/ \
  --content-type "application/javascript" \
  --cache-control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" \
  --metadata-directive REPLACE

# Upload latest versions with shorter cache
aws s3 cp distribution/latest/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/latest/ \
  --content-type "application/javascript" \
  --cache-control "public, max-age=3600" \
  --metadata-directive REPLACE

aws s3 cp distribution/latest/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/latest/ \
  --content-type "application/javascript" \
  --cache-control "public, max-age=3600" \
  --metadata-directive REPLACE
```

### 4.2 Upload Documentation and Examples

```bash
# Create and upload API documentation
aws s3 cp docs/ s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/docs/ --recursive \
  --content-type "text/html" \
  --cache-control "public, max-age=3600"

# Upload examples
aws s3 cp examples/ s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/examples/ --recursive \
  --content-type "text/html" \
  --cache-control "public, max-age=3600"
```

## Step 5: Create CloudFront Distribution

### 5.1 Create Distribution via AWS CLI

Create a CloudFront configuration file:

```json
{
  "CallerReference": "linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn-2024",
  "Comment": "Linkrunner Web SDK CDN Distribution",
  "DefaultCacheBehavior": {
    "TargetOriginId": "linkrunner-web-sdk-s3-origin",
    "ViewerProtocolPolicy": "redirect-to-https",
    "MinTTL": 0,
    "ForwardedValues": {
      "QueryString": false,
      "Cookies": {
        "Forward": "none"
      },
      "Headers": {
        "Quantity": 3,
        "Items": [
          "Origin",
          "Access-Control-Request-Method",
          "Access-Control-Request-Headers"
        ]
      }
    },
    "TrustedSigners": {
      "Enabled": false,
      "Quantity": 0
    },
    "Compress": true
  },
  "Origins": {
    "Quantity": 1,
    "Items": [
      {
        "Id": "linkrunner-web-sdk-s3-origin",
        "DomainName": "linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn.s3.amazonaws.com",
        "S3OriginConfig": {
          "OriginAccessIdentity": ""
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "Enabled": true,
  "PriceClass": "PriceClass_All"
}
```

```bash
aws cloudfront create-distribution --distribution-config file://cloudfront-config.json
```

### 5.2 Create Distribution via AWS Console

1. Go to CloudFront Console
2. Click "Create Distribution"
3. **Origin Settings:**
   - Origin Domain: `linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn.s3.amazonaws.com`
   - Origin Path: (leave empty)
   - Name: `linkrunner-web-sdk-s3-origin`
4. **Default Cache Behavior:**
   - Viewer Protocol Policy: `Redirect HTTP to HTTPS`
   - Allowed HTTP Methods: `GET, HEAD`
   - Cache Based on Selected Request Headers: `None`
   - Compress Objects Automatically: `Yes`
5. **Distribution Settings:**
   - Price Class: `Use All Edge Locations`
   - Alternate Domain Names: (optional - add your custom domain)
   - SSL Certificate: `Default CloudFront Certificate`
6. Click "Create Distribution"

## Step 6: Configure Custom Domain (Optional)

### 6.1 Request SSL Certificate

```bash
# Request certificate for your domain
aws acm request-certificate \
  --domain-name cdn.yourdomain.com \
  --validation-method DNS \
  --region us-east-1
```

### 6.2 Add Custom Domain to CloudFront

1. Go to your CloudFront distribution
2. Click "Edit"
3. Add `cdn.yourdomain.com` to Alternate Domain Names
4. Select your SSL certificate
5. Save changes

### 6.3 Update DNS

Add a CNAME record in your DNS:
```
cdn.yourdomain.com -> d1234567890.cloudfront.net
```

## Step 7: Test CDN Distribution

### 7.1 Test Basic Connectivity

```bash
# Test S3 direct access
curl -I https://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn.s3.amazonaws.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js

# Test CloudFront distribution
curl -I https://d1234567890.cloudfront.net/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js

# Test custom domain (if configured)
curl -I https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js
```

### 7.2 Create Test HTML Page

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>CDN Test</title>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>CDN Distribution Test</h1>
    <div id="status"></div>
    
    <script src="https://d1234567890.cloudfront.net/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js" 
            integrity="sha384-YOUR_SRI_HASH_HERE"
            crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
    
    <script>
        document.getElementById('status').textContent = 
            typeof window.LinkrunnerSDK !== 'undefined' ? 'SDK Loaded Successfully!' : 'SDK Failed to Load';
    </script>
</body>
</html>
```

## Step 8: SDK Consumption Examples

### 8.1 Script Tag Integration (UMD)

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Linkrunner SDK Integration</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Load SDK from CDN -->
    <script src="https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js" 
            integrity="sha384-YOUR_SRI_HASH_HERE"
            crossorigin="anonymous"></script>
    
    <script>
        // Initialize SDK
        async function initLinkrunnerSDK() {
            try {
                const response = await window.LinkrunnerSDK.capturePageView({
                    token: 'your-api-token',
                    package_version: '1.0.0',
                    app_version: '2.0.0',
                    platform: 'web',
                    debug: true,
                    apiUrl: 'https://api.yourdomain.com'
                });
                
                console.log('SDK initialized:', response);
            } catch (error) {
                console.error('SDK initialization failed:', error);
            }
        }

        // Initialize when DOM is ready
        if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
            document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', initLinkrunnerSDK);
        } else {
            initLinkrunnerSDK();
        }
    </script>
</body>
</html>
```

### 8.2 ES Module Integration

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Linkrunner SDK ES Module Integration</title>
</head>
<body>
    <script type="module">
        // Import SDK from CDN
        import { init, getState, isReady } from 'https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js';
        
        async function initializeSDK() {
            try {
                const response = await init({
                    token: 'your-api-token',
                    package_version: '1.0.0',
                    app_version: '2.0.0',
                    platform: 'web',
                    debug: true,
                    apiUrl: 'https://api.yourdomain.com'
                });
                
                console.log('SDK initialized:', response);
                console.log('SDK state:', getState());
                console.log('Is ready:', isReady());
            } catch (error) {
                console.error('SDK initialization failed:', error);
            }
        }

        initializeSDK();
    </script>
</body>
</html>
```

### 8.3 React Integration

```jsx
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';

const App = () => {
    const [sdkReady, setSdkReady] = useState(false);

    useEffect(() => {
        const initSDK = async () => {
            try {
                // Dynamic import from CDN
                const { init, isReady } = await import('https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js');
                
                await init({
                    token: process.env.REACT_APP_LINKRUNNER_TOKEN,
                    package_version: '1.0.0',
                    app_version: process.env.REACT_APP_VERSION,
                    platform: 'web-react',
                    debug: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development',
                    apiUrl: process.env.REACT_APP_API_URL
                });
                
                setSdkReady(isReady());
            } catch (error) {
                console.error('SDK initialization failed:', error);
            }
        };

        initSDK();
    }, []);

    return (
        <div>
            <h1>My App</h1>
            <p>SDK Status: {sdkReady ? 'Ready' : 'Not Ready'}</p>
        </div>
    );
};

export default App;
```

### 8.4 Vue.js Integration

```vue
<template>
  <div>
    <h1>My Vue App</h1>
    <p>SDK Status: {{ sdkReady ? 'Ready' : 'Not Ready' }}</p>
  </div>
</template>

<script>
export default {
  name: 'App',
  data() {
    return {
      sdkReady: false
    };
  },
  async mounted() {
    try {
      const { init, isReady } = await import('https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js');
      
      await init({
        token: process.env.VUE_APP_LINKRUNNER_TOKEN,
        package_version: '1.0.0',
        app_version: process.env.VUE_APP_VERSION,
        platform: 'web-vue',
        debug: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development',
        apiUrl: process.env.VUE_APP_API_URL
      });
      
      this.sdkReady = isReady();
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('SDK initialization failed:', error);
    }
  }
};
</script>
```

### 8.5 Node.js/Server-Side Integration

```javascript
// server.js
import fetch from 'node-fetch';

// For server-side usage, you might want to directly use the HTTP API
async function initLinkrunnerSDK(options) {
    try {
        const response = await fetch('https://api.yourdomain.com/api/client/init', {
            method: 'POST',
            headers: {
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
            },
            body: JSON.stringify(options)
        });
        
        const data = await response.json();
        console.log('Server-side SDK initialized:', data);
        return data;
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Server-side SDK initialization failed:', error);
        throw error;
    }
}

// Usage
initLinkrunnerSDK({
    token: process.env.LINKRUNNER_TOKEN,
    package_version: '1.0.0',
    app_version: '1.0.0',
    platform: 'node',
    device_data: {
        server_name: 'production-server-01',
        node_version: process.version
    },
    install_instance_id: 'server-instance-123'
});
```

## Step 9: Version Management

### 9.1 Deployment Script

Create a deployment script:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# deploy-cdn.sh

VERSION=$1
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    echo "Usage: ./deploy-cdn.sh <version>"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Deploying Linkrunner SDK version $VERSION to CDN..."

# Build the SDK
npm run build:prod

# Create version directory
mkdir -p distribution/v$VERSION

# Copy files
cp dist/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js distribution/v$VERSION/
cp dist/linkrunner-sdk.esm.js distribution/v$VERSION/

# Upload to S3 with versioning
aws s3 cp distribution/v$VERSION/ s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/v$VERSION/ \
  --recursive \
  --content-type "application/javascript" \
  --cache-control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"

# Update latest
aws s3 cp distribution/v$VERSION/ s3://linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn/latest/ \
  --recursive \
  --content-type "application/javascript" \
  --cache-control "public, max-age=3600"

# Invalidate CloudFront cache for latest
aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
  --distribution-id E1234567890ABC \
  --paths "/latest/*"

echo "Deployment complete!"
echo "Version URL: https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v$VERSION/"
echo "Latest URL: https://cdn.yourdomain.com/latest/"
```

### 9.2 Version URLs

```
# Specific versions (immutable, long cache)
https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js
https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.1/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js
https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.1.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js

# Latest version (mutable, short cache)
https://cdn.yourdomain.com/latest/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js

# Major version aliases
https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js
```

## Step 10: Monitoring and Analytics

### 10.1 CloudWatch Metrics

Monitor these CloudFront metrics:
- Requests
- Bytes Downloaded
- Error Rate (4xx, 5xx)
- Cache Hit Rate
- Origin Latency

### 10.2 Real User Monitoring

```javascript
// Add to your SDK usage
window.LinkrunnerSDK.capturePageView(options).then(() => {
    // Track SDK load performance
    if ('performance' in window) {
        const loadTime = performance.now();
        // Send metrics to your analytics
        analytics.track('sdk_load_time', { duration: loadTime });
    }
}).catch(error => {
    // Track initialization errors
    analytics.track('sdk_init_error', { error: error.message });
});
```

### 10.3 CDN Performance Testing

```bash
# Test global performance
curl -w "@curl-format.txt" -o /dev/null -s "https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js"

# curl-format.txt content:
#      time_namelookup:  %{time_namelookup}\n
#         time_connect:  %{time_connect}\n
#      time_appconnect:  %{time_appconnect}\n
#     time_pretransfer:  %{time_pretransfer}\n
#        time_redirect:  %{time_redirect}\n
#   time_starttransfer:  %{time_starttransfer}\n
#                      ----------\n
#           time_total:  %{time_total}\n
```

## Security Considerations

1. **Subresource Integrity (SRI)**: Always use SRI hashes in production
2. **CORS Headers**: Configure appropriate CORS headers
3. **Content Security Policy**: Add CDN domain to CSP
4. **Access Logs**: Enable CloudFront access logs for security monitoring
5. **Rate Limiting**: Implement rate limiting at the origin API level

## Troubleshooting

### Common Issues

1. **404 Errors**: Check S3 bucket policy and file paths
2. **CORS Issues**: Verify CORS headers in CloudFront
3. **Cache Issues**: Use CloudFront invalidation for immediate updates
4. **SSL Issues**: Ensure certificate is properly configured
5. **Performance**: Check CloudFront cache hit rates

### Debug Commands

```bash
# Check CloudFront distribution status
aws cloudfront get-distribution --id E1234567890ABC

# Check S3 bucket policy
aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket linkrunner-web-sdk-cdn

# Test file availability
curl -I https://cdn.yourdomain.com/v1.0.0/linkrunner-sdk.umd.js
```

This completes the comprehensive CDN distribution guide for the Linkrunner Web SDK.
